title: Udhezues procedurat e pajisjes me Vertetim Tatimor
text:
Director General of the Tax Administration of Kosovo,
Based on Article 3 of Law No. 03/L-222 on Tax Administration and Procedures, as amended
by Law No. 04/L-102 and Law No. 04/L-223, issues:
GUIDELINES ON PROCEDURES FOR OBTAINING TAX
CERTIFICATE
Purpose
The purpose of these Guidelines is to inform persons/taxpayers about the procedures to be
followed to obtain a tax certificate, the responsibilities of Taxpayer Service and Education
(TSE) officials in regional directorates to issue a tax certificate, and the responsibilities of
TAK’s Information Technology officials to enable the generation of tax certificate.
Tax Certificate
The Tax Certificate is a document which shows the current tax status of the person/taxpayer,
which is issued by the TAK and used for various purposes.
The Tax Certificate issued by the TAK does not have a stamp/signature, but is a valid
document. The authenticity of the content of the Tax Certificate can be verified at any time
through the TAK’s electronic address http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org.
Institutions that receive the Tax Certificate may verify that the Tax Certificate is identical to
the status of the Tax Certificate of that person/taxpayer at TAK on the date of issue of the
certificate.
Tax certificate generation
The Tax Certificate for natural and business persons is issued by the TAK only in cases when
the taxpayer does not have any outstanding tax liability, as well as in cases where the taxpayer
has outstanding liabilities but is under an arrangement for paying that debt in installments.
The Tax Certificate for natural business persons and legal persons shall be issued under one of
the following conditions:
 The person is registered with the TAK and has no current outstanding tax debts or other
tax liabilities;
Page 1 of 23

 The person is registered with TAK and has current outstanding tax debts but is under
an arrangement for paying that debt in installments.
Whereas, the Tax Certificate for non-natural business persons (individuals) shall be issued by
the TAK only in cases when the person does not have any outstanding tax liability, in cases
when the person has outstanding liabilities but is under an arrangement for paying that debt in
installments, as well as in cases where the TAK has no records of the person.
The Tax Certificate for non-natural business persons (individuals) shall be issued under the one
of the following conditions:
 The person is registered with the TAK and has no current outstanding tax debts or other
tax liabilities;
 The person is registered with the TAK and has current outstanding tax debts but is under
an arrangement to pay the debt in installments;
 The TAK has no records of the person.
Note: The non-business natural person, who does not have a tax account opened with the TAK,
shall be provided with a Tax Certificate at the time of application, depending on his status at
the TAK, such as:
 If the person is on any payroll from the employer in the last 6 years, the Tax Certificate
is generated on the condition that the person be registered with the TAK and has no
current outstanding tax debts or other tax liabilities, or
 If the person is not on any employer payroll in the last 6 years, the Tax Certificate shall
be generated on the condition that the TAK has no records of the person.
Non-generation/Non-issuance of Tax Certificate
The Tax Administration does not generate/issue a Tax Certificate to persons/taxpayers who
have outstanding liabilities, unless the person/taxpayer is under an arrangement for paying the
debt in installments.
The Tax Certificate shall not be issued by the TAK in cases when the person/taxpayer:
 Has outstanding debts;
 Missing any tax return for any type of tax/contribution in the last 6 years;
 The VAT Certificate has been revoked;
 Has passive status;
 The Purchase Book and the Sales Book are missing for any monthly period when the
taxpayer is a VAT declarant. For taxpayers registered in VAT before January 2017 the
obligation to report books is from 1 January 2017, while for taxpayers registered in
VAT after January 2017 the obligation to report books is from the date of registration
for VAT onwards;
 The Purchasing Report over € 500 is missing, when the taxpayer is taxed on real income
(PD or CD). Exceptions to this are taxpayers registered for VAT, who have declared
the Purchase and Sales Book for the entire year (January-December).
Page 2 of 23

Note: Outstanding debts, among others, should be considered when the taxpayer declares the
advance installments and according to the IL or QL form has an obligation to pay. Also, the
absence of the tax return should be considered, among other things, when the taxpayer has not
declared the advance installments with the IL or QL form.
When the person/taxpayer does not meet the above criteria to obtain a Tax Certificate,
Information Technology must enable the generation of the Tax Certificate electronically, and
the message must be submitted which of the above criteria is not met by the taxpayer.
In addition, even in cases where the taxpayer applies for a Tax Certificate manually, while the
person/taxpayer does not meet the above criteria to be provided with a Tax Certificate, the TSE
Office must be sent a message which of the above criteria is not met by the taxpayer. If the
taxpayer does not meet the payment criteria, but the person/taxpayer provides proof of
payments, which are still unprocessed, the Information Technology should enable the
generation of Tax Certificate through the TSE Office.
Application for tax certificate
Application for Tax Certificate can be done electronically through the TAK website (non-
business natural person) and through the EDI Electronic System (business natural person and
legal person). Also, the application can be done manually in the relevant regional office of TSE
(person/taxpayer).
Taxpayer Service and Education (TSE) in the Regional Directorate is obliged to be ready at all
times to provide the person/taxpayer wishing to obtain a Tax Certificate with the necessary
information on how to obtain it. Information on which documents must be submitted in case
of manual application for Tax Certificate must be public and placed in the place where it can
be seen or be readily available to the person/taxpayer.
Obtaining the Tax Certificate online
To obtain the Tax Certificate electronically, the taxpayer must use the Electronic EDI System,
while non-natural business persons can obtain it using the TAK website or the direct link
https://evertetimi.atk-ks.org/.
Obtaining the Tax Certificate online through the EDI electronic system
Through the EDI electronic system, natural business persons and legal persons can be provided
with a Tax Certificate for the following purposes:
 Visa application;
 Tender application;
 Acquisition of citizenship;
 Release from citizenship;
 Maternity leave;
Page 3 of 23

 Extension of the residence permit in Kosovo;
 License application;
 Application for license extension;
 Application for extension of lines;
 Loan application;
 Grant application;
 Application for donation;
 Application for subsidy;
 Application for change of surname;
 Application for customs warehouse.
Where the taxpayer (business) requests a Tax Certificate for any other reason, which is not
listed above, the provision of the Tax Certificate is done only in the relevant office of the TSE,
such as for:
 Business deregistration;
 Changing the form of business;
 Annual turnover;
 Deregistration of the Fiscal Cash Register;
 Other.
Steps to be followed by the taxpayer (natural business person and legal
person) to obtain the Tax Certificate online through the EDI electronic
system
Step 1: Click on www.atk-ks.org to access the website of the Tax Administration of Kosovo,
then click on the link EDI- Deklarimi elektronik (e-filing).
Step 2: After clicking on EDI-Deklarimi Elektronik, the window shown below opens, where
you have to complete the following fields:
 User - enter the Unique Identification Number or Fiscal Number;
 Password - enter the password sent by the TAK upon registration in the EDI system or
the password you changed after it was sent to you by the TAK.
 Code - Enter the code in the message, e.g. 7k6q
After completing the fields, click on Kyçu....(Login)
Page 4 of 23

Step 3: Now that you have accessed the EDI electronic system, click on Shërbimet (Services).
Step 4: After clicking on Shërbimet (services), select the service Vërtetimi Tatimor (Tax
Certificate).
Page 5 of 23

Step 5: Now you have accessed the service Shkarkimi i Vërtetimit Tatimor (download tax
certificate), select Arsyen e lëshimit të Vërtetimit Tatimor (reason of requesting tax
certificate).
Step 6: After clicking to select Arsyen e lëshimit të Vërtetimit Tatimor (reason of requesting
tax certificate), you will be shown types of tax certificates downloadable through the EDI
electronic system. Where you need a Tax Certificate for other reasons not listed in the table
above, such Tax Certificate is only issued at the relevant TSE office.
Page 6 of 23

Step 7: After selecting the reason for requesting the Tax Certificate, e.g. tender application,
click on Gjenero Vërtetimin (Generate Certificate).
Step 8: After clicking on Gjenero Vërtetimin (Generate Certificate), the requested certificate
will be generated as follows:
Page 7 of 23

Page 8 of 23

Online verification of tax certificate
Step 1: Go to http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org.
Step 2: After opening http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org, click on Kërko Vërtetimin (Request
Certificate).
Page 9 of 23

Step 3: After clicking on the Kërko Vërtetimin (Request Certificate) link, enter the serial
number of the Tax Certificate issued electronically. For example, enter the serial number of the
Certificate generated above: 1000735971 and then click on the Kërko (Search) button.
Step 4: After clicking on the Kërko (Search) button you will be shown the Tax Certificate data
at the time of issuance by TAK, such as: Personal Number, Person’s Name, Municipality,
Date, Reason for Issuance, Tax Status and Validity.
Page 10 of 23

Obtaining the Tax Certificate online through the TAK’s website
Through the TAK’s website, non-natural business persons can be provided with a Tax
Certificate for the following purposes:
 Visa application;
 Acquisition of citizenship;
 Release from citizenship;
 Maternity leave;
 Extension of the residence permit in Kosovo;
 Loan application;
 Grant application;
 Application for donation;
 Application for subsidy;
 Application for change of surname;
In cases when the non-business natural person requests a Tax Certificate for other reasons not
listed above, the provision of the Tax Certificate is done only in the relevant TSE office, such
as:
 Other.
Steps to be followed by non-natural business persons to obtain a Tax
Certificate online through the TAK’s website or directly through the link
https://evertetimi.atk-ks.org/
Step 1: Open www.atk-ks.org log on to the website of the Tax Administration of Kosovo. Once
logged in, click on the Vërtetimi Tatimor (Tax Certificate) link or you can access the link
directly https://evertetimi.atk-ks.org/
Page 11 of 23

Step 2: After clicking on the Vërtetimi Tatimor (Tax Certificate) link or on the
https://evertetimi.atk-ks.org/ link, the following window opens:
You must have an open account in order to log in and be able to download the Tax Certificate.
If you have an account then click on the Kyçu (Login) button; if not, click on the Regjistrohu
(Register) button.
Step 3: After clicking the Regjistrohu (Register) button the following window appears, where
you are required to fill in all the fields:
Page 12 of 23

Step 4: After completing all the fields, reading the terms of the contract and clicking on the
Regjistrohu (Register) button, the confirmation message “Registration completed successfully,
you will receive the confirmation by email” is displayed.
Step 5: The password is sent to the user's e-mail written in the required field during registration.
Step 6: After clicking on the Kyçu (Login) button the following window appears, where you
have to fill in the fields:
 User - enter the personal number
 Password - enter the password sent in the confirmation email.
Once you have filled in the required fields, click on the Kyçu (Login) button.
Page 13 of 23

Step 7: The natural person has accessed the application for downloading the Tax Certificate.
1000000000, Personi X
personi@domain.com
Step 8: In the following window click on the Shkarko Vërtetimin (Download Certificate)
button where the window for filling in the required fields for downloading the Tax Certificate
appears.
1000000000, Personi X
personi@domain.com
Personi X
1000000000
Page 14 of 23

Step 9: In addition to the Person’s Name, Personal Number and Municipality fields, which are
displayed automatically, the user must also fill in the reason for request and fields for other
reasons of request if there are any. Click on the Gjenero Vërtetimin (Generate Certificate)
button to download the Tax Certificate.
1000000000, Personi X
personi@domain.com
Personi X
1000000000
Page 15 of 23

Step 10: After clicking on the Gjenero Vërtetimin (Generate Certificate) button, the required
Certificate will be generated as follows:
Personi X
1000000000
600000000
Page 16 of 23

Online verification of Tax Certificate
Step 1: Go to http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org.
Step 2: After logging on to http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org, click on the link Kërko Vërtetimin
(Request Certificate).
Step 3: After clicking on the Kërko Vërtetimin (Request Certificate) button, write the serial
number of the Tax Certificate downloaded electronically. E.g. write the serial number of the
Certificate generated above: 1000736006, then click on the Kërko (Search) button.
Page 17 of 23

Step 4: After clicking on the Kërko (Search) button you will be shown the Tax Certificate data
at the time of issuance by TAK, such as: Personal Number, Person’s Name, Municipality,
Date, Reason for Issuance, Tax Status and Validity.
1000000000, Personi X
personi@domain.com
1000000000
Personi X
Page 18 of 23

Obtaining the Tax Certificate manually
The person/taxpayer, in order to obtain a Tax Certificate, applies at the relevant regional office
of TSE. The TSE office verifies that the data required in the application are complete and
accurate. The relevant regional office of TSE is required to file the request for Tax Certificate
together with the accompanying documentation, including the status of the taxpayer printed at
the time of application for Tax Certificate, as well as the Tax Certificate issued by TAK.
The TSE office issues a certificate confirming that he/she has applied for a Tax Certificate to
any taxpayer who cannot be provided with a Tax Certificate at the time of application due to
verification of their condition by the Operations Department.
Required documentation to apply for Tax Certificate manually
1. To apply for a Tax Certificate manually, the taxpayer must possess the following
documents:
 Request for Tax Certificate (received at TSE regional office or the TAK’s
website);
 Copy of the owner’s/manager’s ID card or copy of the authorization from the
owner/manager if the Tax Certificate is requested by the representative on
behalf of the taxpayer;
 General balance (balance must hold the date of the Tax Certificate application).
Note: If the Tax Certificate is requested for Business Deregistration and Change of Business
Form, such certificate can be obtained by the taxpayer only when the taxpayer submits
(returns):
o Original Fiscal Number Certificate (if any)
o Original VAT certificate (if any).
2. To apply for a Tax Certificate manually, a non-business natural person must
possess the following documents:
 Request for Tax Certificate (received at TSE regional office or the TAK’s
website);
 Copy of the person’s ID card/passport or copy of the authorization from the
person if the Tax Certificate is requested by the representative on behalf of the
person. Persons who do not have an ID card/passport should submit a copy of
their birth certificate/extract;
 General balance (if there is a balance with the TAK, the balance must hold the
date of the Tax Certificate application).
Page 19 of 23

Page 20 of 23

What should the TSE official have in mind when receiving the request for
Tax Certificate?
During the verification of the status of the person/taxpayer on the SIGTAS system base, when
it is identified that any declaration of taxes or contributions is missing, while the party provides
the original declaration, proving that he has declared taxes/contributions, then the TSE official
is authorized to verify the original statement and photocopy it in two (2) copies. A copy of the
tax return is attached to the application and a copy forwarded to the Processing Division
(regional operator) for processing.
If the person/taxpayer requesting the Tax Certificate has no evidence that he has completed the
tax returns for tax accounts in the last six (6) years, as well as the required reporting (such as:
purchase book, sales book, purchase report over 500, etc., if required), then it is suggested that
he should first complete the tax returns and bring the evidence related to the returns, in order
for them to be attached to the application, and then the application to be accepted by TSE office.
If the person/taxpayer has made tax returns for tax accounts in the last six (6) years, and there
is no other outstanding tax liability, the TSE Office issues the Tax Certificate. Also, even in
cases when the taxpayer has completed all tax returns, however, there are outstanding liabilities
(debt), then if the taxpayer has entered into an agreement with TAK to pay those debts in
installments, the Tax Certificate can be issued by TAK. Exception to this, TSE should not issue
the Tax Certificate with outstanding debts even though the taxpayer may be in agreement for
payment of that debt in installments, in cases where the taxpayer requests the Tax Certificate
with the reason for Business Deregistration, Change of business form, Deregistration of the
Fiscal Cash Register, etc.
Note: TSE officials in the Regional Directorates are responsible for receiving the
request/application and issuing the Tax Certificate. For applications completed as required by
these Guidelines, the TSE should have no reason for delays.
Taxpayer’s obligations
In cases when the taxpayer is in debt but has also made advance payments, to be provided with
a Tax Certificate, the taxpayer must make a request for debit-credit compensation in advance
and such a request must be processed in the system. So, after the situation in the system is
regulated, the taxpayer is provided with a Tax Certificate without obligations to the TAK.
Note: Until the application is fully completed with the documents required to obtain a tax
certificate, it will not be accepted by the official of the relevant TSE Office. Also, if the
person/taxpayer makes a mistake while filling out the application, it is not allowed for the error
to be corrected with a proofreader, but the new application must be completed.
Issuance of certificate
Page 21 of 23

When the person/taxpayer submits the completed application as required, the TSE official
prints the necessary certificate for the person/taxpayer.
The Tax Certificate is not stamped, it is signed by the TSE official. The Tax Certificate is a
valid document as it is considered to have an electronic stamp and signature. The authenticity
of the content of the Tax Certificate can be verified at any time through the TAK’s electronic
address at http://evertetimi.atk-ks.org.
Before being given to the party, a copy of the Tax Certificate must be stored (archived) in the
relevant TSE office.
In case of withdrawal (receipt) of the certificate by the taxpayer, when the purpose of the Tax
Certificate is Deregistration of business or Change of business form, the party must sign the
protocol book, enter the name and surname, identification number and data other necessary.
The Tax Certificate shall be issued by the TAK to a natural business person and legal
person upon one of the following conditions:
 The person is registered with the TAK and has no outstanding tax debts or other tax
liabilities, or
 The person is registered with the TAK and is currently under an arrangement for
paying the outstanding tax debt in installments.
The Tax Certificate shall be issued by the TAK to a natural non-business person upon
one of the following conditions:
 The person is registered with the TAK and has no outstanding tax debts or other tax
liabilities, or
 The person is registered with the TAK and is currently under an arrangement for
paying the outstanding tax debt in installments, or
 The TAK has no records of the person.
Obtaining a Tax Certificate despite the tax obligations of the
person/taxpayer
The person/taxpayer can be provided with a Tax Certificate even if he/she has current
outstanding tax debts to the TAK, only if he has entered into an agreement with TAK for the
payment of that debt in installments. Exceptionally, the taxpayer who requests the Tax
Certificate with the reason for Business Deregistration, Change of business form or
Deregistration of the Fiscal Cashier, cannot be provided with a Tax Certificate without paying
all tax obligations.
In the case of non-natural business persons who are employed and whose tax liabilities to the
TAK pertain to the employer, the employee must be provided with a Tax Certificate despite
the fact that the employer has outstanding tax liabilities to TAK.
Where can the Tax Certificate be obtained?
Page 22 of 23

In order to facilitate the procedures and provide better and faster services by the TAK, natural
non-business persons can be provided with a Tax Certificate online through the TAK website
or through the link https://evertetimi.atk-ks.orgor at any of the nearest TSE regional office.
Natural business persons and legal persons (businesses) can obtain a tax certificate online
through the EDI electronic system or in the respective TSE regional office.
Tax Certificate Validity
The validity periods for tax certificates are as follows:
Validity period of Tax
Purpose
Certificate
Visa application 6 months
Tender application 90 days
Acquisition of citizenship 90 days
Release from citizenship 90 days
Maternity leave 90 days
Extension of the residence permit in Kosovo 90 days
License application 90 days
Application for license extension 90 days
Application for extension of lines 90 days
Loan application 90 days
Grant application 90 days
Application for donation 90 days
Application for subsidy 90 days
Application for surname change 90 days
Application for customs warehouse 90 days
Business deregistration 10 days
Changing the form of business 7 days
Annual turnover 90 days
Deregistration of the Fiscal Cash Register 10 days
Other 90 days
Transitional provisions
These Guidelines abrogate all other guidelines regulating this matter.
Entry into force
These Guidelines enter into force on 01 November 2020.
Ilir Murtezaj
Date 14/10/2020
Director General of TAK
Page 23 of 23
